prompt: I need to assess the production readiness of WebTransport as a replacement for WebSockets in real-time applications. The research should cover browser support matrices, server-side implementations (Go, Rust, Node.js), QUIC transport layer considerations, and migration strategies from existing WebSocket infrastructure. I also need benchmarks comparing WebTransport vs WebSocket latency and throughput under load.

assertions.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories (e.g., MDN, GitHub, Google, caniuse)
Search queries use engine-specific operators such as language:, stars:>50, or site: filters
Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
At least one query targets browser compatibility or feature detection data
At least one query targets latency or throughput benchmarks comparing WebTransport and WebSocket
Matrix includes queries for server-side WebTransport implementations in at least 2 languages
